note: Bullet text may contain restricted HTML (<strong>, <em>, <code>, <a>). Author-controlled — sanitize at the edit step, not at render.

sections.heading: Status page — launched
bullets: Stood up nenni26.netlify.app as the public change log. Source is at github.com/garyricke/nenni26; Netlify auto-deploys on every push to main.

Built as a sibling directory nenni26-status/ with four files: updates.json (source of truth), index.html (the page you're reading), embed.js (Squarespace embed), and netlify.toml (no-build config + CORS headers).

Squarespace install is a one-time snippet: <div id="nenni-status"></div><script src="https://nenni26.netlify.app/embed.js"></script>. The embed script auto-discovers its own data URL from script.src origin — no manual config.
updates.json is served with Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * and a 60-second cache, so new pushes propagate to Squarespace within ~1 minute.

sections.heading: Ep 144 (Charlie Zitnik) — title & audio refresh
bullets: Episode title finalized as "Why Energy Projects Stall — and How to Fix It." Hero h1 kept punchy as "Why energy projects stall." to preserve the two-line / bold-word visual rhythm; full title used in the audio-player title and on the social thumbnail.

Audio replaced with the Descript magic-edit clean pass: 32:36 → 31:37, ~59 seconds shorter via non-uniform trims spread throughout the timeline. Updated <audio src> to the new Squarespace CDN URL and refreshed the hardcoded #bep-audio-total and #bep-audio-left placeholders.

All 14 chapter timestamps re-anchored individually from the Descript-exported transcript. Could not use a constant offset since the magic-edit trims are non-uniform — every chapter shifted by a different amount (e.g. t: 480 → 489 but t: 720 → 684).

Show notes timestamps re-computed and rounded down to the nearest :00 / :30 so click-throughs land just before each topic transition (never mid-sentence after). Locked this convention into project memory.

Vertical 9:16 social thumbnail regenerated via nano-banana with the new "Why Energy Projects Stall" headline and "— and how to fix it · with Charlie Zitnik, D.A. Davidson" subhead. Same three-band layout as ep143.

sections.heading: Steve Goehl — spelling correction
bullets: Charlie's partner is Steve Goehl (pronounced "Gale"). Descript transcribed it phonetically as "Gale" — fixed all 4 instances in ep144-zitnik.html: timeline summary, timeline body, the "what gets you out of bed" Signature Four card, and the bio modal paragraph.
Added a general guardrail to project memory: when a guest references a colleague or partner by name, verify spelling with Gary before treating the transcript as ground truth.

sections.heading: Short description & show notes — rewritten
bullets: Short description (itunes:summary) rewritten to lead with the new title: "Why energy projects stall — and how to fix it." followed by the round-table / day-one closer. First sentence stands alone, so the hook survives the ~270-char preview truncation most apps apply.

Full show notes "In This Episode" timestamps updated to the cleaned audio: 04:00 → 05:30 → 08:00 → 10:00 → 11:00 → 14:00 → 18:00 → 21:30 → 25:00 → 27:00 → 29:30. Housekeeping chapters (welcome, legal disclaimer, career intro) intentionally omitted from the bullet list — only topical meat gets a timestamp.

sections.heading: Ep 144 (Zitnik) — source materials ingested
bullets: Full Descript transcript (Charlie-Zitnik-D-A-Davidson-full-transcript.md) and source MP3 (Charlie-Zitnik-D-A-Davidson-may-7-2026-full-descript.mp3) saved under episodes/Charlie-Zitnik-D-A-Davidson-may-7-2026/.

Established the per-episode source-asset folder convention: raw materials (transcripts, source audio, headshot, reference docs) live under episodes/{episode-folder}/; the landing-page HTML stays at the project root as ep{NNN}-{lastname}.html.
First-pass social thumbnail (charlie-zitnik-thumb.jpeg) generated for layout testing — would be superseded by the published version on 2026-05-21.

sections.heading: Ep 143 — social thumbnail finalized
bullets: 9:16 vertical social thumbnail generated for Episode 143 (David Karlsgodt / Brailsford & Dunleavy) via nano-banana.

Established the three-band layout convention for all future BEP thumbnails: top wordmark + episode pill on navy; middle portrait floated on navy gradient with faint architectural-elevation texture; bottom near-black panel with wedge-serif headline + sans subhead + hairline rule + tiny "Hosted by Jim Schafer · Nenni & Associates" credit.
Palette locked in: navy #1a2a3a, steel-blue #5b8db8, off-white headline #f7f8fa, near-black type panel #1a1c21.

sections.heading: Ep 143 (David Karlsgodt) — first per-episode landing page
bullets: Built ep143-karlsgodt.html as the first instance of the deep-dive per-episode pattern, distinct from the all-episodes archive. Squarespace code-block embed (no <html>/<head>/<body>); every CSS rule scoped to #bep-ep143 to avoid Squarespace theme collisions.

Custom HTML5 audio player (not Squarespace's .sqs-audio-embed — too restrictive): animated 4-bar equalizer that dances on play, 64px steel-blue play button, ±15s skip buttons, scrubbable track with chapter tick-marks, elapsed/remaining time, 1× → 2× speed toggle, collapsible chapter list below.

Standard section order locked in: hero (dark gradient + Cloudinary headshot card) → custom audio player → stats strip → career timeline → pull quote → video clip 1 → tabs section → pull quote 2 → accordion FAQ → video clip 2 → Signature Four reveal cards → concept-primer modals.

Google Fonts Raleway (display caps) + Source Sans 3 (body) loaded via @import at top of the scoped <style> block. Defensive #bep-ep143 h1,h2,h3 { color: inherit; } rule to defeat Squarespace heading-color overrides; explicit color: #fff on dark-section headings.

sections.heading: HTML encoding — three-contexts rule established
bullets: Codified a rule for non-ASCII characters inside Squarespace code-block embeds: HTML entities in body (—, ·), \uXXXX in JS string literals ('\u2014'), \HHHH in CSS pseudo-content (content: '\2192').
Affects em dash, middle dot, right arrow, ×, −, ▾, é, smart quotes — never blanket-replace; the escape required depends on which context the character sits in.

sections.heading: Brand guide — standalone reference doc
bullets: Built brand-guide.html as a self-contained reference for the Nenni & Associates brand identity used across the Building Efficiency Podcast site.

Captured the canonical palette, type pairing (Raleway caps + Source Sans 3 body + wedge serif for editorial headlines), Cloudinary asset-naming conventions, and the WSS / BEP / BPAI prefix scheme on the shared dsbllwpbh cloud account.
